sharedRootCause: The flood overlay's operator-visible acreage is rendered from several independent surfaces — the FEMA finding's free-text flood.summary, report.reconciliation.flood, report.govDd.flood.note, the workStatus 'Flood overlay' item, and the floodCard detail — each of which embedded the acreage captured at research time, when the overlay area was sampled against a provider/assessed acreage (1.32) rather than the queried GIS geometry (1.15). The first repair only recomputed flood.zones[].approximateAcres and left every free-text surface unchanged, so the 100% Zone-X card still displayed 1.32 ac on a 1.15 ac parcel, now without even the overlay-exceeds-geometry warning (which had been silenced because floodOverlayAcres was forced to 1.15).
whyAutomatedTestsMissedIt: checkOverlayConsistency and the zone-array normalization were unit tested, but no test asserted the FREE-TEXT flood surfaces (summary/reconciliation/govDd/workStatus) against the mapped geometry, and no test tied all flood-acreage surfaces to a single pinning helper. The partial fix passed its own tests while leaving the string surfaces wrong.
whyBrowserQaMissedIt: The first browser QA correctly caught the persisted 1.32 in the zone table; the partial repair moved the number in one field but not the summary/reconciliation strings, which only an adversarial recheck reading the actual rendered flood card and the /report payload surfaced.
missingInvariant: Every operator-visible flood/overlay acreage surface (summary, reconciliation.flood, govDd.flood.note, workStatus, decisionCard detail) MUST derive from the mapped GIS geometry via the single shared pinOverlayAcresToGeometry helper; no free-text surface may embed a persisted provider/assessed overlay acreage.
missingAcceptanceJourney: Read the rendered FEMA flood card text AND report.reconciliation.flood AND govDd.flood.note on the acceptance card and assert none states an acreage exceeding the mapped geometry (a 100% zone reads ~1.15 ac everywhere).
sharedRepair: One shared helper pinOverlayAcresToGeometry(zones, mappedAcres) in acreage-basis.ts feeds EVERY flood-acreage surface: (1) the operator record pins flood zones AND regenerates flood.summary (so floodCard detail + workStatus inherit it) on the /report path; (2) routes.ts projectPublicScreening pins zones before composing reconciliation.flood.primary and govDd.flood.note; (3) a new routes.ts pinRunOverlaysToGeometry(run) pins the flood task's zones+summary at read time for the /public-intelligence endpoint that feeds the DD Public Property Intelligence panel; and (4) public-property-intelligence-live samples overlay area against the queried geometry at the source for new runs. The recurrence lesson (a partial per-field fix) was resolved by routing ALL surfaces through the one helper across BOTH endpoints (/report and /public-intelligence).
newRegressionTest: src/landos/acreage-basis.test.ts 'pinOverlayAcresToGeometry (WS1 F1 regression)': 100% zone pins to mapped geometry, partial zone scales by percentage, null geometry leaves zones untouched, and a pinned zone never fails checkOverlayConsistency against the geometry.
newBrowserAssertion: On the acceptance card the FEMA flood card, report.reconciliation.flood.primary, and govDd.flood.note all state ~1.15 ac (never 1.32 ac) for a 100% Zone-X overlay, and no overlay exceeds the mapped parcel; verified across a hard refresh.
